title: nicolasia coronata subsp. coronata
intro: Nicolasia coronata subsp. coronata, also known as the Yellow Nicolasia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Polynesian cultures, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Nicolasia coronata subsp. coronata include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and wound healer. Nicolasia coronata subsp. coronata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
